text:
Once upon a time in a quiet village lived an old owl named Oliver. Every dawn he would listen to the whispers of the wind through the trees. One particular summer evening, the skies glowed a rich purple.

"Oh what a beautiful night," Oliver thought.

He flew down to the meadow. There were stars everywhere! He had never seen anything like it. Then he spotted a tiny figure beside a birch tree. It was a young fox.

"I wish you wings like me," said the fox. "You could soar through the stars!"

"I could hardly fly," sighed Oliver. "But my eyes see every twinkling."

The fox thought. "That's just like you. You see wonders where others see darkness."

Oliver flew up high. Night owls are nocturnal! He glided silently through stars. He saw moons big as disks.

"Those are probably stars," the fox said gently. "But you've seen worlds invisible except to you."

"You think so?"
